headline_seed: Consumer prices rose 3.8% annually in April, the highest since May 2023 - CNBC
draft_text:
CPI rose 3.8% year-over-year in April — highest since May 2023, per the Bureau of Labor Statistics. Gasoline up 28.4% over the year. CNBC reports real wages fell, with inflation outpacing pay for the first time since 2023.

And that's the mews.
